element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all subsequent movement and obstacle avoidance
Primary objective mechanic; six items per area
Primary antagonist; cannot be affected by most distraction items
Temporarily stops Meowkies; stacks up to 15 items
Causes Meowkies to lose consciousness; stronger effect than Cat Toys
Distracts Goro specifically; complements Cat Toys for dual-enemy control
Attracts both Goro and Meowkies; different tactical use case
Required collection of specific items; optional bonus subareas yield extra lives
Spawned as time pressure; cannot be distracted or attacked; forces rapid completion
Stage-specific tools; flashlight wards off ghosts; balloon enables movement
Triggered by short jump; replaces door mechanics from original Mappy
Narrative wrapper; alters target items but maintains core gameplay

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After completing all 8 areas in a story
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Timed delivery challenge; must collect items and reach Mapico/Mappy Jr. before music ends

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully using distraction items (Cat Toys, Silver Vine Pots)
Reaching exit after collecting all six target items
Entering bonus subareas with extra lives
Completing a story and progressing to next narrative chapter
typical_tension_triggers: Appearance of Goro and Meowkies in close proximity
Time warning chime and music speed-up
Spawn of Goro Coin (unavoidable, unkillable pursuer)
Entering subarea sections where specific items must be located
Final castle timed delivery sequence (Story completion)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation within each area (safe collection → enemy pressure → time stress). Story transitions provide narrative pacing relief. Later stories significantly increase enemy speed, creating difficulty curve progression. Ghost Town subarea callback to original Mappy provides nostalgia relief break.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus subareas accessible by jumping on specific trampolines with no controller input; signaled by successful trampoline trigger, not visual cue
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Ghost Town subareas sometimes echo original Mappy game layout with nostalgic theme music as hidden reference; standard subarea warps redirect to different doorways

difficulty_curve.early_game: Story 1 in Railroad Town serves as tutorial; basic enemy patterns, ample time to collect six items, straightforward layouts. Introduces core mechanics without pressure.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stories 2-3 (Western World through Pirate World): Stage layouts become more complex, Meowkies increase in number, timing requirements tighten. Distraction item management becomes strategic. Bonus subareas introduce optional challenge.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Story 4 (Jungle World through Milky Town): Enemy speeds significantly increase, stage layouts alter substantially, final castle bonus area adds strict time limit and delivery mechanic. Loops back to Story 1 with compounded difficulty.
notable_spikes: Time warning chime triggering Goro Coin spawn (immediate threat escalation)
Final castle bonus sequence in Milky Town (timed delivery under music countdown)
Story 4 and beyond: Documented re-runs with 'much faster' enemies and altered layouts
Jungle World: Exception to trampoline breaking mechanics creates unexpected rule shift

notable_level_patterns.description: Each story cycles through 8 distinct themed areas (Railroad Town, Western World, Tropical World, Jungle World, Pirate World, Ghost Town, Seventh Avenue, Milky Town). Goro wears corresponding costume per theme. Areas feature stage-specific counterattack items and unique mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Railroad Town (Story 1, Area 1) - introductory; Ghost Town (Area 6) - mechanics variation

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must collect six target items in main area, then sometimes locate and enter a subarea to obtain a special item before exiting. Subarea access varies by story and area; some require specific conditions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Variable across all stories; Ghost Town subareas occasionally recreate original Mappy level design

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard completion allowed under normal time. If time expires, warning chime sounds, music speeds up, and Goro Coin spawns as unkillable pursuer. Forces rapid item collection on subsequent attempts.

Four narrative stories provide thematic context for target items (Mapico's birthday cheese, wedding rings, Christmas trees, Mappy Jr.'s baseballs). Core gameplay identical; story loops after Story 4 completion with increased difficulty and altered layouts.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Story transitions between Mapico's Birthday (Story 1) → Wedding (Story 2) → Christmas (Story 3) → Jr.'s Birthday (Story 4)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Final castle bonus area (Milky Town completion)
notable_level_patterns.description: After collecting all items in final area of a story, player enters a castle bonus stage. Must collect six target items and deliver them to Mapico or Mappy Jr. before music ends. Requires successful completion to advance; endless retries in NES version until success. Awards bonus points.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Milky Town, final sequence per story

notable_level_patterns.description: Player can carry up to 15 distraction items (Cat Toys, Gold Coins, Silver Vine Pots, Fish). Strategic deployment to control enemy behavior. Different items affect different enemies (Cat Toys → Meowkies, Gold Coin → Goro, Silver Vine Pots → Meowkies unconscious, Fish → both). Bonus subareas require zero inventory to access.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All areas; inventory pressure increases in later stories

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Mappy-Land (Regional Differences, hidden level select, bonus area mechanics)
